description: PromptPal Phase 1 CLI — a bash+Python3 tool that improves prompts via Claude CLI or Anthropic API backends, with multi-turn refinement, local history, WSL2 support, and winget distribution.

userStories.title: Create project skeleton and Backend ABC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core directory structure and Backend abstract base class in place so all subsequent stories have a stable foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure exists: bin/, core/, tests/unit/, tests/integration/, defaults/
core/backend.py defines Backend ABC with name, complete(system, messages, stream), and check_auth() methods
core/__init__.py, core/config.py (Config dataclass with all fields from SPEC §4/§10) exist
defaults/config.json exists with all default field values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/intelligent-tu-0cbc1b after reconciling two parallel Ralph passes (this branch and feat/execute-ralph-script-TN3Xk on origin/main) against SPEC.md from origin/r1. Both drafts drifted from spec; this cherry-pick restores compliance. Backend ABC (SPEC §6): name as @property, complete(system, messages: list[dict], stream: bool = False) → BackendResponse (frozen dataclass with text/input_tokens/output_tokens for the API/CLI null-tokens distinction per P1-BKND-10), check_auth() → bool. NoBackendError(Exception) with MESSAGE class constant matching the §6 remediation text verbatim, exported so US-006 can raise it. Config dataclass (SPEC §10): exactly 10 fields — version, default_model, default_iterations, auto_copy, show_diff, system_prompt_path, history_enabled, max_history_entries (500), system_prompt_update_url, preferred_backend. system_prompt_update_url defaults to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/z0rd0n88/PromptPal/main/core/system_prompt.txt (project-specific override of SPEC's empty-string default). defaults/config.json mirrors the dataclass exactly; tests enforce field-set parity and value parity in both directions. Load/save/merge plumbing intentionally deferred to US-002. 15 tests pass; pyright clean.

userStories.title: Implement Config loading with merge order
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Config to load defaults, then config.json overrides, so all components get correct runtime configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Config.load() merges: dataclass defaults → ~/.promptpal/config.json overrides → CLI flag overrides
Unknown fields in config.json are silently ignored
Type-mismatched fields fall back to default with a stderr warning
Corrupt config.json (JSON parse error) → exit 1 with 'Config file corrupt at ~/.promptpal/config.json. Delete it to reset.'
Config.preferred_backend accepts exactly 'auto' | 'claude-cli' | 'api-key'; other values fall back to 'auto' with stderr warning
Writes to config.json use atomic tempfile.mkstemp → os.rename pattern
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-002-config-loading. Adds load/merge/atomic-save plumbing on top of the spec-compliant 10-field Config from US-001. Public API: ConfigCorruptError, normalize_preferred_backend, apply_overrides, load_config, save_config. AC #1 merge order is realized by apply_overrides being the single chokepoint used by load_config (file overrides on Config()) and by the future CLI entry point (flag overrides on the file-merged Config). AC #2 unknown fields silently dropped. AC #3 type mismatches keep base's value and warn on stderr; strict bool/int distinction via type(value) is bool/int (Python's isinstance(True, int) caveat). AC #4 ConfigCorruptError carries the canonical 'Config file corrupt at <path>. Delete it to reset.' message for the CLI to surface verbatim; the library never sys.exit()s directly. AC #5 preferred_backend clamped through normalize_preferred_backend, which also runs from apply_overrides so the invariant holds at every layer. AC #6 save_config uses tempfile.mkstemp+os.replace in the same dir as the target path for same-filesystem atomic rename; UTF-8, LF newlines, trailing newline; cleanup on failure. 33 new tests (41 total in tests/unit/test_config.py); pytest 48/48 green across the project; pyright clean.

userStories.title: Implement core/platform.py — detect_platform and clipboard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want platform detection and clipboard support so the tool behaves correctly on macOS, Linux, and WSL2.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_platform() reads /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease; sets is_wsl=True when 'microsoft' found, wsl_version=2 when 'wsl2' found
HOME guard: if is_wsl=True and HOME starts with /mnt/c/ or /c/, print WSL launch fix message to stderr and exit 1
_resolve_home() cross-checks HOME against passwd entry; passwd wins when HOME is NTFS-mounted
Clipboard provider priority: xclip → xsel → pbcopy → clip.exe (WSL only) → none
clip.exe sends UTF-8 without BOM; verified for non-ASCII characters
When no clipboard provider is available, copy_to_clipboard() prints one-line warning to stderr and returns without error
All files written by PromptPal use LF line endings
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform. Public surface: Platform (frozen dataclass: is_wsl, wsl_version, home, clipboard_cmd), detect_platform(), assert_wsl_home_safe(platform), copy_to_clipboard(text, platform) → bool, and constants WSL_LAUNCH_FIX_MESSAGE / NO_CLIPBOARD_WARNING / OSRELEASE_PATH the tests pin verbatim. Internal helpers _detect_wsl / _resolve_home / _detect_clipboard exposed for direct unit testing (each takes a parameter that makes the test inject inputs without monkeypatching globals). AC #1 _detect_wsl reads /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease lowercase; 'microsoft' → is_wsl=True; 'wsl2' substring → wsl_version=2 else 1; missing/unreadable → (False, None). AC #2 assert_wsl_home_safe checks the *raw* env HOME (not the already-resolved one) so the guard fires even when _resolve_home succeeded; prints WSL_LAUNCH_FIX_MESSAGE to stderr + sys.exit(1). AC #3 _resolve_home falls back to pwd.getpwuid(os.getuid()).pw_dir only when is_wsl and HOME starts with /mnt/c/ or /c/; KeyError on passwd lookup degrades gracefully to env HOME. AC #4 _detect_clipboard probes shutil.which in tuple-priority order xclip → xsel → pbcopy → clip.exe (gated on is_wsl) → (). AC #5 copy_to_clipboard sends text.encode('utf-8') — no BOM; verified for Greek/CJK/emoji round-trip in test_copy_to_clipboard_utf8_no_bom. AC #6 missing provider → exactly one stderr line NO_CLIPBOARD_WARNING; non-zero exit / OSError → stderr warn, return False, never raise. AC #7 regression test confirms save_config writes LF only. 33 new tests (81 total project tests); pytest green; pyright clean; 100% line coverage of core/platform.py.

userStories.title: Implement ApiBackend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ApiBackend to call the Anthropic Messages API so users with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY can improve prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: ApiBackend implements Backend ABC against POST /v1/messages with anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
API key read only from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var; never written to disk, logs, or stdout
Retry: 429 honors Retry-After (max 3 retries); 5xx exponential backoff 1s/2s/4s (max 3); network errors retry once after 2s; 401 fails immediately
complete() returns full assistant text; streams tokens to terminal when stream=True and stdout is a TTY
check_auth() performs minimal messages call with max_tokens=1
Each turn records backend: 'api-key', input_tokens, output_tokens as numeric values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003 not yet merged to main when this iteration ran, so this story stacks on top of it). Module: core/api_backend.py. Public surface: ApiBackend (Backend subclass), ApiError + ApiAuthError/ApiRateLimitError/ApiServerError/ApiNetworkError/ApiKeyMissingError, plus canonical-message constants (NO_KEY_MESSAGE, AUTH_ERROR_MESSAGE, RATE_LIMITED_MESSAGE_TEMPLATE, SERVER_ERROR_MESSAGE, NETWORK_ERROR_MESSAGE) that tests pin verbatim. AC #1 POSTs to {base_url}/v1/messages with headers x-api-key + anthropic-version=2023-06-01 + Content-Type=application/json. AC #2 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read only in __init__; an empty/missing env raises ApiKeyMissingError carrying NO_KEY_MESSAGE (P1-ERR-01); tests assert the key value never appears in repr/str/stdout/stderr even on auth failure. AC #3 retry budgets are independent counters per category: 429→up to 3 retries honoring integer Retry-After (default 1s), 5xx→up to 3 retries with 1s/2s/4s backoff, network→1 retry after 2s, 401→no retry. Test test_retry_categories_have_independent_counters proves a 429 burst doesn't exhaust the 5xx budget. AC #4 do_stream = bool(stream) and sys.stdout.isatty(); when both true the SSE stream is consumed and text_delta chunks are written to stdout as they arrive; otherwise the request is non-streaming and the full text comes from a single JSON parse — either path returns BackendResponse(text=..., input_tokens, output_tokens). AC #5 check_auth sends a 1-token call with messages=[{role:user,content:'ping'}] and returns False on any ApiError (auth, rate-limit-exhausted, server-exhausted, network-exhausted). AC #6 input_tokens/output_tokens are int values on success (set from message_start + message_delta usage events in streaming, from .usage in non-streaming). HTTP layer is injectable via the transport= kwarg (Callable returning _HttpResponse / raising _TransportNetworkError) — tests use a FakeTransport without touching urllib; the default transport is a urllib-based POST that also covers SSE iteration. Sleeper is injectable via the sleeper= kwarg so retry tests record sleep durations instead of waiting. 45 new tests in tests/unit/test_api_backend.py; full project 126 tests green; pyright clean; coverage 99% on core/api_backend.py (97% across core/). Streaming uses message_start (input_tokens, partial output_tokens) and message_delta (final output_tokens) to assemble usage; only content_block_delta/text_delta events contribute to the text body.

userStories.title: Implement CliBackend with stream-json pipe
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CliBackend to invoke the claude CLI via stream-json so multi-turn context is preserved natively without role-prefix hacks.
acceptanceCriteria: CliBackend invokes: claude --print --model <m> --bare --system-prompt-file <path> --input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json
messages array is fed as NDJSON on stdin; assistant/message events consumed from stdout
Auth failure detected via non-zero exit + auth keyword in stderr → 'Claude CLI auth failed. Run: claude auth login'
check_auth() runs claude --version
Each turn records backend: 'claude-cli', input_tokens: null, output_tokens: null
A prompt containing the literal substring 'Human:' produces a correct improved prompt (no role-prefix confusion)
3 successive iterations send all 5 prior messages entries as NDJSON on turn 3
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003/US-004 not yet merged to main when this iteration ran, so US-005 stacks on top of them). Module: core/cli_backend.py. Public surface: CliBackend (Backend subclass); CliError + CliAuthError/CliNotFoundError/CliInvocationError; canonical-message constants AUTH_ERROR_MESSAGE ('Claude CLI auth failed. Run: claude auth login') and CLI_NOT_FOUND_MESSAGE ('Error: claude CLI not found on PATH. Install Claude Code first.') that tests pin verbatim; AUTH_FAILURE_KEYWORDS = ('authentication','unauthorized','auth','login','token') matching P1-BKND-09; STREAM_JSON_FLAGS pinned in tuple form. AC #1 _build_argv returns the full D-10 vector ['claude','--print','--model',<m>,'--bare','--system-prompt-file',<path>,'--input-format=stream-json','--output-format=stream-json'] in that exact order; the test pins each slot by index so silent reordering breaks the suite. AC #2 _serialize_messages_ndjson encodes the messages list as one JSON object per line, UTF-8, LF, ensure_ascii=False (so emoji/CJK ride the wire as raw bytes, not \uXXXX) and terminates with a single trailing newline; empty list → empty bytes. AC #3 auth detection: _is_auth_failure lowers stderr and looks for any keyword; CliBackend.complete maps non-zero exit + keyword → CliAuthError(AUTH_ERROR_MESSAGE), non-zero exit without keyword → CliInvocationError including the truncated stderr (≤500 chars, '<no stderr>' placeholder when empty). Exit 0 with auth-shaped stderr is NOT classified as auth failure. AC #4 check_auth runs [executable,'--version'] with empty stdin; returns True on exit 0, False on any non-zero / CliNotFoundError / CliError — never raises. AC #5 BackendResponse always carries input_tokens=None and output_tokens=None for CLI turns (P1-BKND-10). AC #6 stream-json parser preserves role boundaries structurally, so a user-content payload containing the literal substring 'Human:' survives round-trip byte-identical (regression bait for the dropped _build_prompt flattener). AC #7 turn 3 of 3 successive complete() calls sees all 5 prior NDJSON entries — the test explicitly walks the user→assistant→user→assistant→user accumulation that the future refinement loop (US-010) will perform. System prompt is written to a 0600 tempfile per call (mkstemp + LF), path passed via --system-prompt-file, cleaned up in a finally block even when the runner raises. The subprocess layer is injected via the runner= kwarg (Callable returning _CliRunResult / raising); the default runner translates FileNotFoundError → CliNotFoundError so the CLI surface can print the SPEC §12 message verbatim. _parse_stream_json supports both the canonical {type:assistant,message:{content:[{type:text,text:...}]}} envelope and the streaming {type:content_block_delta,delta:{type:text_delta,text:...}} shape for forward-compat with claude versions; non-text blocks, non-assistant envelopes, blank lines, and unparseable lines are silently skipped. 60 new tests in tests/unit/test_cli_backend_streamjson.py (matches the US-016 P1-BKND-03 mapping); full project 186 tests green; pyright clean; coverage 98% on core/cli_backend.py (97% across core/). The stream kwarg is accepted for ABC parity but is a no-op on the CLI path — P1-PIPE-07 is SHOULD and lives with the pipeline (US-009/US-010).

userStories.title: Implement resolve_backend and backend persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want backend resolution with explicit-flag persistence so users who pick a backend keep using it without re-specifying it each run.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_backend(preferred, model) selection order: explicit flag → Config.preferred_backend → auto-detect (claude on PATH → CliBackend; else ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set → ApiBackend; else NoBackendError)
When preferred == 'claude-cli' and claude missing → FileNotFoundError with actionable text; no fallback to API
When preferred == 'api-key' and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset → env-var instructions message; no fallback to CLI
When neither backend available → exit 1 with two-option setup message containing 'Option 1 (Claude CLI):' and 'Option 2 (API key):'
--backend <claude-cli|api-key> persists to config.preferred_backend after first successful turn only
--backend auto resets config.preferred_backend to 'auto' immediately
Failed call does not persist backend choice
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (still stacking on top of US-003/US-004/US-005 since none have merged to main yet). Module: core/resolve.py. Public surface: resolve_backend(preferred, model, *, config, env=None, which=None, api_factory=None, cli_factory=None) -> Backend, plus clear_backend_preference(config_path) and persist_backend_preference(config_path, value). Constants: CLAUDE_EXECUTABLE='claude', ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_ENV='ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'. The error hierarchy lives in core.cli_backend (CliNotFoundError → CLI_NOT_FOUND_MESSAGE) and core.api_backend (ApiKeyMissingError → NO_KEY_MESSAGE); resolve.py just raises them at the right gate. NoBackendError (core.backend) carries the two-option setup block already mandated by SPEC §6. AC #1 selection ladder is realized by an internal _effective_preference(preferred, config_pref) that collapses (CLI flag, Config) into the single 'effective' value before the env/PATH gates run: explicit 'claude-cli'/'api-key' always wins; preferred=None + valid config_preference falls through to config; everything else (None+invalid, 'auto', unknown typo) collapses to 'auto'. AC #2 / P1-BKND-05 — preferred='claude-cli' with claude missing on PATH raises CliNotFoundError(CLI_NOT_FOUND_MESSAGE) even when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set; tests pin that no silent fallback to API occurs. AC #3 / P1-BKND-06 — preferred='api-key' with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset (or empty string) raises ApiKeyMissingError(NO_KEY_MESSAGE) even when claude is on PATH. AC #4 / P1-BKND-07 — both unavailable + auto raises NoBackendError whose MESSAGE contains both 'Option 1 (Claude CLI):' and 'Option 2 (API key):' lines verbatim (AC-BKND-05 regression bait). AC #5 / AC #6 / AC #7 — persistence is split between two helpers driven by the future CLI layer: clear_backend_preference writes 'auto' immediately (before resolution) when --backend auto is parsed; persist_backend_preference writes 'claude-cli' or 'api-key' after the first successful turn. Failed call → caller's try/except skips the persist call → disk untouched (the contract is structural, not enforced by a guard inside the persist helper). Both helpers use load_config + replace + save_config, so other Config fields are preserved verbatim and the atomic-write contract (P1-CFG-05) is inherited for free. Missing config file → fresh write with defaults + the override field. Hooks: env=os.environ by default, which=shutil.which by default, api_factory/cli_factory default to the real ApiBackend/CliBackend constructors (tests substitute stub subclasses to avoid the env-key requirement on ApiBackend.__init__). 33 new tests: 14 in tests/unit/test_backend.py (resolve_backend selection + error paths, P1-BKND-04..07), 19 in tests/unit/test_backend_persistence.py (NEW — persist/clear semantics, atomic-write inheritance, AC-BKND-04 end-to-end). Full project 217 tests green; pyright clean (after wiring pytest into the typecheck env via .ralph-runner.sh — a US-005 learning that resurfaced); coverage 100% on core/resolve.py, 98% across core/.

userStories.title: Implement system prompt management
userStories.description: As a developer, I want system prompt seeding and update logic so the bundled default is always available and updates are integrity-verified.
acceptanceCriteria: core/system_prompt.txt exists with a default prompt
On first run (no ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md), installer seeds from core/system_prompt.txt
~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md is never overwritten without explicit --update-system-prompt

--update-system-prompt downloads URL + .sha256 sidecar, verifies hash, atomically replaces ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md; on hash mismatch → exit 1 with 'checksum mismatch' message and existing file unchanged
Before atomic replace, current file is backed up to ~/.promptpal/system-prompt.md.bak with path printed
--system-prompt FILE overrides resolved path for current invocation only (no persistence)
If system_prompt_path missing or unreadable → exit 1 with actionable message pointing to --update-system-prompt
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003..US-006 not yet merged to main when this iteration ran, so US-007 stacks on top of them). Module: core/system_prompt.py. Public surface: seed_system_prompt(target, *, bundled_path) -> bool; resolve_system_prompt_path(config, *, cli_override) -> Path; read_system_prompt(path) -> str; update_system_prompt(url, target, *, fetcher) -> Path|None; SystemPromptError + SystemPromptMissingError / SystemPromptChecksumError / SystemPromptDownloadError; canonical-message constants MISSING_MESSAGE_TEMPLATE, CHECKSUM_MISMATCH_MESSAGE (P1-ERR-14), DOWNLOAD_FAILED_MESSAGE_TEMPLATE (P1-ERR-15), BUNDLED_SYSTEM_PROMPT_PATH, BACKUP_SUFFIX ('.bak'), SHA256_SIDECAR_SUFFIX ('.sha256'). All canonical messages pinned verbatim by tests. AC #1 core/system_prompt.txt is a UTF-8/LF Prompt Builder-style default (7 numbered principles). AC #2 seed_system_prompt streams via shutil.copyfile so bundled bytes round-trip exactly; creates parent directory on demand. AC #3 seed_system_prompt is a no-op when target exists (returns False), even for empty files -- realizes the P1-SP-02 no-overwrite guarantee. AC #4 update_system_prompt fetches url + url + '.sha256', computes hashlib.sha256(payload).hexdigest(), compares against parsed sidecar; on mismatch raises SystemPromptChecksumError and target is untouched. _parse_sidecar accepts both bare-hex ('<hex>\n') and sha256sum coreutils format ('<hex>  <filename>\n'), lowercases, validates 64-hex; malformed sidecar collapses to checksum-mismatch (a malformed sidecar cannot authorize an overwrite). AC #5 if target exists, current bytes copied to <target>.bak BEFORE the atomic replace; backup path is RETURNED so the CLI surface owns the print-decision (cleaner than coupling to stderr inside this module). A second update overwrites the prior .bak (single rolling backup). AC #6 resolve_system_prompt_path returns Path(cli_override).expanduser() when given, else config.resolved_system_prompt_path(); cli_override is NOT persisted -- Config is not mutated, verified by test_resolve_does_not_persist_override. AC #7 read_system_prompt translates FileNotFoundError / IsADirectoryError / PermissionError / OSError -> SystemPromptMissingError with MISSING_MESSAGE_TEMPLATE.format(path=...) so the CLI layer prints the canonical actionable message verbatim and exits 1. Atomic replace: _atomic_write_bytes uses tempfile.mkstemp(prefix='.system-prompt-', dir=target.parent) + os.replace -- same-FS atomic rename; tempfile unlinked in except on os.replace failure (no leak). target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) called inside _atomic_write_bytes so a first-run --update-system-prompt invocation can run directly into a missing ~/.promptpal/ directory. Default fetcher (_default_fetcher) is urllib-backed and translates HTTPError/URLError/OSError -> SystemPromptDownloadError with DOWNLOAD_FAILED_MESSAGE_TEMPLATE; tests inject a programmable fake fetcher (dict[url, bytes]) so the network is never touched. Production caller passes nothing and gets the urllib default. 51 new tests in tests/unit/test_system_prompt.py covering all 7 ACs + atomic-write semantics + sidecar format tolerance + _default_fetcher error translation + happy path via mocked urlopen. Full project 268 tests pass; pyright clean; coverage 98% on core/system_prompt.py (98% across core/). The 2 uncovered lines are the nested OSError catch inside the tempfile-cleanup branch of _atomic_write_bytes -- defensive code that's hard to trigger deterministically.

userStories.title: Implement history and persistence layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want session JSON files and an index so users can search, replay, and review past prompt refinements.
acceptanceCriteria: One JSON file per session at ~/.promptpal/history/<uuid>.json with schema per SPEC §4
~/.promptpal/history/index.json maintained with fields: session_id, created_at, label, status, original_prompt_preview
All writes use atomic tempfile.mkstemp → os.rename; temp file cleaned up on any write failure
Session file updated on every turn with status: 'in-progress'; finalized to 'accepted' or 'discarded' on loop exit
Append-only NDJSON usage.log with one line per turn; input_tokens/output_tokens may be null
Config.max_history_entries enforced by evicting oldest entries from index and disk after every accept/discard
History write OSError is non-fatal: warns to stderr, run continues, exit code unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003..US-007 not yet merged to main when this iteration ran, so US-008 stacks on top of them). Two new modules + one refactor: core/_io.py (NEW shared helpers), core/history.py (NEW US-008 surface), core/config.save_config + core/system_prompt._atomic_write_bytes refactored to delegate. Public surface of core/history.py: Session / Turn / IndexEntry (frozen dataclasses with to_dict/from_dict roundtrip), STATUS_IN_PROGRESS / STATUS_ACCEPTED / STATUS_DISCARDED constants, new_session / append_turn / finalize_session (immutable updates), write_session / read_session / index_entry_from_session / upsert_index_entry / read_index / write_index, enforce_max_entries, append_usage_entry, search_history, warn_history_failure, plus HistoryError + SessionNotFoundError. Canonical constants HISTORY_WRITE_WARNING ('Warning: could not save session to history.'), INDEX_FILE_NAME ('index.json'), USAGE_LOG_NAME ('usage.log'), ORIGINAL_PROMPT_PREVIEW_LIMIT (80) pinned by tests. AC #1 / P1-HIST-01: write_session lands at <history_dir>/<session_id>.json via session_path helper; uuid hex naming (32-char lowercase) when id_factory is the default. AC #2 / P1-HIST-02: IndexEntry has exactly session_id / created_at / label / status / original_prompt_preview; index_entry_from_session derives it from Session; the on-disk index.json is a JSON array (not a dict) ordered newest-first by created_at. AC #3 / P1-HIST-03: every JSON write goes through core/_io.atomic_write_bytes which now lives in one place (replacing the duplicated mkstemp+replace blocks in config.save_config and system_prompt._atomic_write_bytes). The tempfile prefix is per call site ('.session-', '.index-', '.config-', '.system-prompt-') so leftovers from a different module's failure don't masquerade as ours. Cleanup-on-failure inherited from the helper. AC #4 / P1-HIST-04: Session/Turn are frozen dataclasses; append_turn returns a new Session via dataclasses.replace with the new Turn appended and updated_at advanced. write_session is called both for incremental in-progress writes and the final accept/discard write — same path, file overwritten in place (no proliferation). finalize_session refuses 'in-progress' / unknown statuses so a caller bug can't ever write a non-terminal status as terminal. AC #5 / P1-HIST-05: append_usage_entry appends one NDJSON line per turn to usage.log; input_tokens/output_tokens carry through as None (JSON null) for CLI turns and as int for API turns. Append-only (not atomic) is intentional — NDJSON's one-record-per-line shape means a kill -9 truncates at most the last record. AC #6 / P1-HIST-06: enforce_max_entries reads the index, sorts newest-first by created_at, keeps the first max_entries, unlinks the rest's session files (tolerating FileNotFoundError so a half-evicted state can recover), and rewrites the index. max_entries=0 evicts everything; max_entries<0 is treated as 'unlimited' so a caller bug can't wipe history. AC #7 / P1-HIST-07: search_history matches the index's label and original_prompt_preview first (case-insensitive substring), then falls back to scanning original_prompt and final_prompt in the session files for index-misses. Missing/corrupt session files during the fallback are skipped silently — search must not crash the run. Empty keyword returns every entry newest-first. AC #8 / P1-HIST-08: warn_history_failure(error=None) prints the canonical 'Warning: could not save session to history.' to stderr; the helper is provided so callers' try/except blocks have one canonical message to surface. The library never sys.exit()s — non-fatality is the caller's contract. Refactor side: extracted atomic_write_bytes(target, payload, *, prefix, suffix) and append_ndjson_line(path, record) into core/_io.py; config.save_config and system_prompt._atomic_write_bytes now delegate; the os.replace monkeypatches in test_system_prompt.py moved from 'core.system_prompt.os.replace' to 'core._io.os.replace' (two-line test edit). 64 new tests in tests/unit/test_history.py covering all 8 ACs + lifecycle integration + dict roundtrip + UTF-8 preservation + atomic-write contract inheritance. Full project 332 tests pass (was 268 before US-008); pyright clean; coverage 100% on core/history.py, 92% on core/_io.py (2 lines: nested OSError catch in tempfile cleanup, same defensive code as before the refactor), 99% across core/.

userStories.title: Implement prompt input resolution and diff display
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pipeline to accept prompts from CLI args, stdin, or interactive TTY, and show a unified diff for long outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: promptpal "your prompt" reads prompt from positional arg
echo "prompt" | promptpal reads from stdin when stdin is not a TTY
When no arg and stdin is a TTY, prompt user for interactive input
Unified diff displayed between original and improved prompt when improved length > 3 lines
Final improved prompt written to stdout; all logs, diff chrome, spinner go to stderr
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003..US-008 still stacked on this branch). Two new modules: core/input.py (prompt input resolution) and core/diff.py (unified diff display). Public surface of core/input.py: read_prompt(positional_arg, *, stdin=None, stderr=None, isatty=None) -> str; EmptyPromptError(ValueError) carrying EMPTY_PROMPT_MESSAGE; constants EMPTY_PROMPT_MESSAGE and INTERACTIVE_PROMPT_BANNER pinned verbatim by tests. Public surface of core/diff.py: line_count(text) -> int; should_show_diff(improved) -> bool; format_diff(original, improved, *, fromfile, tofile) -> str; constants DIFF_LINE_THRESHOLD (=3), DEFAULT_FROMFILE ('original'), DEFAULT_TOFILE ('improved'). AC #1 / P1-PIPE-01: positional_arg non-None and non-whitespace -> returned verbatim (byte-identical so 'Human:' regression bait from AC-MT-01 survives this layer too); whitespace-only or empty-string arg raises EmptyPromptError (NOT a silent fallthrough to stdin -- caller-bug detection). AC #2 / P1-PIPE-02: positional_arg None + isatty()==False -> stdin.read() returned verbatim (no trim of trailing newline, so multi-line piped payloads round-trip clean); empty/whitespace-only pipe raises EmptyPromptError. AC #3 / P1-PIPE-03: positional_arg None + isatty()==True -> INTERACTIVE_PROMPT_BANNER written to stderr THEN stdin.read() returned (Ctrl-D / Ctrl-Z+Enter semantics); banner never lands on stdout, upholding the P1-PIPE-09 pipe-safety contract even in interactive mode. AC #4 / P1-PIPE-06: should_show_diff is len(improved.splitlines()) > 3 -- splitlines treats trailing-newline-present and trailing-newline-absent identically so 'a
b
c' and 'a
b
c
' are both 3 lines and BOTH fall under the threshold. Boundary tests pin 3-line=False, 4-line=True. format_diff uses difflib.unified_diff with lineterm='' so the joined output has no double-newline gaps; identical inputs return ''. AC #5 / P1-PIPE-09: neither module writes anything to stdout -- read_prompt's interactive banner goes to stderr; format_diff returns the diff string as a value and the CLI layer (US-011) is responsible for writing it to stderr. Test seam pattern matches the rest of core/: stdin/stderr/isatty are injectable kwargs, defaults are sys.stdin/sys.stderr/stdin.isatty; tests use io.StringIO without monkeypatching sys globals. 47 new tests (28 in tests/unit/test_diff.py covering boundary + format markers + UTF-8 + custom filenames; 19 in tests/integration/test_stdin.py covering all 3 input modes + UTF-8 round-trip + empty-prompt detection + default-isatty fallback). Full project 379 tests green (was 332 before US-009); pyright clean; coverage 100% on core/diff.py and core/input.py, 99% across core/.

userStories.title: Implement refinement loop (accept/iterate/discard/raw/copy)
userStories.description: As a user, I want an interactive refinement loop so I can accept, iterate on, or discard the improved prompt in a single session.
acceptanceCriteria: After displaying improved prompt, show choice line: [a]ccept [i]terate [d]iscard [r]aw [c]opy
[i]terate reads user feedback, appends as user turn, calls Backend.complete(), appends response, re-displays; full messages array sent every call
At minimum 3 successive iterations supported in a single session
[a]ccept writes session JSON with status: 'accepted', copies to clipboard if auto_copy or --copy, exits 0
[d]iscard writes session JSON with status: 'discarded', exits 0
[r]aw prints raw improved prompt to stderr (not stdout), re-presents choice line
[c]opy copies current improved prompt to clipboard, re-presents choice line; warns non-fatally if no provider
--iterations N runs N auto-iterations with synthesized feedback turn before presenting choice line
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (US-003..US-009 still stacked on this branch). Module: core/loop.py. Public surface: run_refinement_loop(*, backend, system, initial_messages, initial_improved, original_prompt, auto_iterations=0, copy_on_accept=False, stdin=None, stderr=None, display=None, choice_reader=None, feedback_reader=None, copy_fn=None) -> LoopOutcome; LoopOutcome (frozen dataclass: status, final_prompt, messages, new_turns); LoopTurn (frozen dataclass: backend, input_tokens, output_tokens, response_text, user_content); canonical-message constants CHOICE_PROMPT ('[a]ccept [i]terate [d]iscard [r]aw [c]opy: '), ITERATE_FEEDBACK_PROMPT, SYNTHESIZED_FEEDBACK ('Improve this further.'), RAW_HEADER ('--- raw improved prompt ---'), COPY_SUCCESS_MESSAGE ('Copied to clipboard.'); action constants ACTION_ACCEPT/ITERATE/DISCARD/RAW/COPY (single chars); STATUS_ACCEPTED/STATUS_DISCARDED matching history's; VALID_ACTIONS tuple. AC #1 / P1-LOOP-01: CHOICE_PROMPT names all 5 actions verbatim; default choice_reader writes it to stderr before stdin.readline(). AC #2 / P1-LOOP-02: [i]terate appends a user turn with the feedback, calls backend.complete(system, full messages list copy), appends assistant response — full conversation sent every call, no truncation. Test pins the 3rd call's payload as 7 messages (initial 2 + 2 prior u/a pairs + new user feedback). AC #3 / P1-LOOP-03: test_three_iterations_in_one_session exercises [i,i,i,a] with backend response queue depth 3 and asserts all 3 backend calls landed. AC #4 / P1-LOOP-04: [a]ccept returns LoopOutcome(status=STATUS_ACCEPTED, final_prompt=...); when copy_on_accept=True AND copy_fn is wired, calls copy_fn(improved) and prints COPY_SUCCESS_MESSAGE on True; on False the copy_fn is responsible for its own warning (platform.copy_to_clipboard prints NO_CLIPBOARD_WARNING) so we don't double-print. AC #5 / P1-LOOP-05: [d]iscard returns LoopOutcome(status=STATUS_DISCARDED, final_prompt=...) carrying the *latest* improved text so the historical record reflects what the user saw. AC #6 / P1-LOOP-06: [r]aw prints RAW_HEADER + improved to STDERR (not stdout, upholding P1-PIPE-09) and re-presents the choice line. AC #7 / P1-LOOP-07: [c]opy calls copy_fn; when copy_fn=None prints 'Warning: no clipboard provider available.' to stderr (matches P1-PLAT-07 non-fatal contract); always re-presents the choice line. AC #8 / P1-LOOP-08: auto_iterations=N runs N synthesized 'Improve this further.' rounds BEFORE the choice line, with exactly one display() call after the final auto-iteration. auto_iterations<0 treated as 0 (defensive default — never crash on a caller typo). The loop does NOT write session JSON, does NOT call sys.exit, does NOT call platform.copy_to_clipboard directly — persistence and exit codes are the CLI layer's job (US-011); clipboard is injected via copy_fn so tests don't need a real provider. Pipe-safety enforced structurally: zero stdout writes anywhere in the module (verified by capsys assertion). The loop also defends against a misbehaving backend that mutates its messages argument — _iterate passes a defensive copy. Choice normalization is case-insensitive and first-char-only ('accept yes' → 'a', 'iterate now' → 'i'). EOF on stdin (readline returns '') bails as discard so a closed input stream cannot infinite-loop the suite. AC-MT-01 'Human:' regression bait survives the loop's messages projection byte-identical (no role-prefix flattening). 41 new tests in tests/integration/test_pipeline.py covering all 8 ACs plus defensive paths (typo'd choice, EOF, case insensitivity, mutating backend, alias safety). Full project 420 tests green (was 379 before US-010); pyright clean; coverage 100% on core/loop.py (99% across core/).

userStories.title: Implement CLI argument parsing (core/cli.py)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all flags parsed via argparse into a CLIOptions dataclass so every feature has a clean programmatic interface.
acceptanceCriteria: core/cli.py uses argparse; all flags from PRD §5.4 are wired: --model, --iterations, --no-history, --copy, --show-history, --replay, --system-prompt, --output, --quiet, --search, --export, --name, --update-system-prompt, --uninstall, --backend, --status
--output accepts plain | json | markdown; json emits single object {original, improved, turns, session_id, backend, model}
--quiet suppresses diff/spinner/streaming/choice; emits only improved prompt on stdout; auto-accepts after first turn
--show-history prints paginated list from index.json newest-first then exits 0
--search KEYWORD matches index original_prompt_preview and label first; falls back to scanning session files; results sorted by created_at descending
--status prints backend, model, auth check, platform, config path, history count in < 20 lines; exits 0
--export SESSION_ID dumps full session JSON to stdout and exits 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch feat/us-003-platform (stacked on US-003..US-010 not yet merged to main). Module: core/cli.py. Public surface: CLIOptions (frozen dataclass with 17 fields, one per PRD §5.4 flag), build_parser, parse_args, format_output, cmd_status, cmd_show_history, cmd_search, cmd_export, cmd_update_system_prompt, _platform_label, _config_overrides_from_options, _short_backend_name, and main(argv, **seams) -> int. AC #1: argparse wires every PRD §5.4 flag including --model/--iterations/--no-history/--copy/--show-history/--replay/--system-prompt/--output/--quiet/--search/--export/--name/--update-system-prompt/--uninstall/--backend/--status; choices on --output and --backend are pinned by tests. AC #2: format_output emits the six-key envelope (original/improved/turns/session_id/backend/model) for --output json; the 'backend' field uses the canonical short form via _short_backend_name (P1-BKND-10) — the human-readable full name still appears in --status output. AC #3: --quiet path bypasses the refinement loop, runs first turn + N synthesized iterations (when --iterations N), writes session with status=accepted, emits ONLY the improved prompt on stdout. AC #4: cmd_show_history prints newest-first (sorted by created_at desc) with a 20-row page; longer indexes get a truncation footer noting older sessions; empty index → '(no history yet)' single line. AC #5: cmd_search delegates to history.search_history (index-first, session-body fallback), results sorted newest-first. AC #6: cmd_status — first line 'Platform: WSL2|WSL1|Linux|macOS' per P1-PLAT-09; lines for Backend, Model, Auth, Config, History; <20 lines (NFR-09); auth-failure surfaces as 'Auth: failed' instead of crashing; no-backend-available shows 'Backend: (unavailable)' with Reason and still exits 0 (the whole point of --status). AC #7: cmd_export dumps full session JSON to stdout via Session.to_dict (indent=2, ensure_ascii=False); missing session → Error: session 'X' not found. on stderr, exit 1. Wired but stubbed in this story (US-015 owns the real impl): --uninstall and --replay both exit 1 with canonical UNINSTALL_NOT_IMPLEMENTED / REPLAY_NOT_IMPLEMENTED messages that tests pin. --update-system-prompt threads through to system_prompt.update_system_prompt with the injected fetcher; canonical P1-ERR-14 / P1-ERR-15 messages surface verbatim. Pipe-safety contract (P1-PIPE-09) is upheld: stdout receives only improved-prompt (plain), JSON envelope, or report output; all diff chrome / loop banners / error messages go to stderr. Test seam pattern: main() accepts config_path, history_dir, usage_log_path, stdin/stdout/stderr, detect_platform_fn, backend_factory, fetcher, clock, id_factory, copy_fn, skip_wsl_guard — production callers pass nothing, tests run the full pipeline without touching the real filesystem, network, env, or subprocess. The session.backend / Turn.backend / usage-log backend fields use the canonical short form ('claude-cli'/'api-key') derived via _short_backend_name() — Backend.name returns the full descriptive 'claude-cli (model)' form for --status display, but P1-BKND-10's session schema wants only the first token; the JSON envelope's 'backend' field follows the same short-form rule for consistency. Loop iterations (interactive [i]terate, auto-iterations) each append one usage-log line per P1-HIST-05 — fixed during this US so it doesn't regress into US-016. 77 new tests in tests/unit/test_cli.py; full project 497 tests green; pyright clean; coverage 86% on core/cli.py (96% across core/).

userStories.title: Implement bin/promptpal bash entrypoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want a bash entrypoint that guards against WSL HOME regression and delegates to the Python core so the tool is a single command on PATH.
acceptanceCriteria: bin/promptpal is a bash script that delegates to python3 -m core.main
Entrypoint duplicates the WSL HOME guard (HOME starts with /mnt/c/ or /c/) and exits 1 with the WSL fix message before Python starts
shellcheck bin/promptpal returns clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement install.sh
userStories.description: As a user, I want install.sh to set up ~/.promptpal/ and place the binary on PATH so I can start using the tool immediately after running the one-liner.
acceptanceCriteria: install.sh works on macOS, Linux, and WSL2 Ubuntu without extra runtime installs beyond bash, python3, curl/wget
Installer refuses to run when HOME is /mnt/c/... or /c/...; prints wsl -d Ubuntu fix and exits 1
Binary placed at $INSTALL_DIR/promptpal (default ~/.local/bin/promptpal); warns if not on PATH
~/.promptpal/ created with history/, config.json (from defaults/config.json), and system-prompt.md (from core/system_prompt.txt); existing files never overwritten
Post-install backend check runs and prints which backend(s) are configured; if none, prints two-option setup hint (non-fatal, exits 0)
shellcheck install.sh returns clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement winget Windows launcher
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want a winget-distributed launcher so I can run promptpal from PowerShell without managing WSL manually.
acceptanceCriteria: launcher/promptpal.exe (or promptpal.bat / promptpal.ps1) checks for WSL Ubuntu; if absent, prints 'wsl --install -d Ubuntu' instruction and exits 1
When WSL Ubuntu is present, forwards all args via wsl -d Ubuntu -- promptpal "$@"
Launcher contains no Anthropic logic
winget manifest exists and passes winget validate
Given WSL Ubuntu installed and PromptPal installed inside it, running promptpal from PowerShell returns the improved prompt to PowerShell stdout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement uninstall.sh and --replay flag
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cleanly uninstall the tool and replay past sessions so I can maintain my environment and continue refining saved prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: uninstall.sh removes the installed binary
uninstall.sh prompts (Yn) before removing ~/.promptpal/; --purge removes without prompting
--replay SESSION_ID loads specified session, replays messages into a new session, enters refinement loop
--name LABEL assigns human-readable label recorded in session file and index
shellcheck uninstall.sh returns clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire full test suite and CI matrix
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the complete test suite passing at ≥80% coverage across all required test files so the codebase is releasable.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/unit/test_backend.py covers all resolve_backend paths (P1-BKND-01..09)
tests/unit/test_cli_backend_streamjson.py verifies stdin NDJSON, command vector with --bare --system-prompt-file --input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json
tests/unit/test_backend_persistence.py covers --backend api-key persists, --backend auto clears, failed call does not persist
tests/unit/test_system_prompt.py covers bundled-default seed, no-overwrite on existing, sidecar verify, mismatch refusal
tests/unit/test_platform.py covers WSL detection, HOME guard, clipboard priority, UTF-8 round-trip
tests/unit/test_history.py covers atomic write, index upsert, eviction, search, incremental update
tests/unit/test_config.py covers defaults, file override, CLI override, corrupt file, unknown-field tolerance
tests/unit/test_diff.py covers short prompt → no diff; long prompt → unified diff
tests/integration/test_stdin.py covers arg, stdin, and interactive input paths
tests/integration/test_pipeline.py covers all loop branches with mock backend
tests/integration/test_flags.py covers one assert per flag
pytest --cov=core reports ≥ 80% coverage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
